prompt:
Hi, i had a simular problem. Here is the tips that helped me.
1) Manage your time! Use special apps and calendars. You can find this apps at Play Market.

2) Don't spend a lot of time to TV and social media. You should leave from useless groups at VK, don't watch YouTube a lot!

3) Sleep more than 8 hours and eat good food. It will bring you enought energy.

I hope, my tips will help you. For more information, you can write me at personal messages.

TASK: You read this post in the Telegram chat of first year students: It is so difficult to study in university! I don't have any free time at all! Write an answer to this post (10 sentences) and give some tips on how to organise your days to have more free time.
STUDENT_TEXT: Hi, i had a simular problem. Here is the tips that helped me.
1) Manage your time! Use special apps and calendars. You can find this apps at Play Market.

2) Don't spend a lot of time to TV and social media. You should leave from useless groups at VK, don't watch YouTube a lot!

3) Sleep more than 8 hours and eat good food. It will bring you enought energy.

I hope, my tips will help you. For more information, you can write me at personal messages.
TEXT_TYPE: essay

Write an answer to this post (10 sentences) and give some tips on how to organise your days to have more free time.","student_text":"Hi, I know some people who have the simular problem. So, let me try to give you few tips about solving it.

Firstly, the most important thing is time managment. You need to understand when you sleep, eat, go for a walk, etc.

Then, I think it's very nice to solve problems when they was given for you. Don't put them closely to deadlines. If you don't do it, you will have to make many tasks in one-two days. It's surely not good.

My final tip is about eating healthy food. Yes, this tip is really obvios, but without it you probably will have a lot of health difficulties.

So, I hope my tips will help you to get more free time! And it's interesting for me to know, what do you think about it?","expert_annotation":{"annotator_id":8,"errors":[{"text":"simular","labels":["Spelling"],"start":36,"end":43},{"text":"few","labels":["Grammar"],"start":80,"end":83},{"text":"managment","labels":["Spelling"],"start":150,"end":159},{"text":"nice","labels":["Lexical choice"],"start":250,"end":254},{"text":"for","labels":["Grammar"],"start":293,"end":296},{"text":"was","labels":["Grammar"],"start":283,"end":286},{"text":"put them","labels":["Lexical choice"],"start":308,"end":316},{"text":"closely to deadlines.","labels":["Lexical choice"],"start":317,"end":338},{"text":" one-two","labels":["Grammar"],"start":394,"end":402},{"text":"obvios","labels":["Spelling"],"start":499,"end":505},{"text":"you probably will ","labels":["Sentence structure"],"start":522,"end":540},{"text":" what do you think about it?","labels":["Sentence structure"],"start":668,"end":696},{"text":" know, what","labels":["Punctuation"],"start":662,"end":673}],"strengths":[{"text":"Firstly","labels":["Discourse markers ✓"],"start":108,"end":115},{"text":"My final tip ","labels":["Discourse markers ✓"],"start":432,"end":445},{"text":"probably ","labels":["Rhetorical features ✓"],"start":526,"end":535},{"text":"Then","labels":["Discourse markers ✓"],"start":226,"end":230}],"comments":[["significant"]]}},{"ref_id":"sample_07016","cefr_level":"B2","task":"You read this post in the Telegram chat of first year students: It is so difficult to study in university! Write an answer to this post (10 sentences) and give some tips on how to organise your days to have more free time.","student_text":"Lack of free time is a popular problem among students. But if you learn how to manage your time property, you will be able to work effectively and have time for hobbies and rests. The following tips helped me with that :

1) The main thing is not procces of planning itself, but the purpose of planning, because this purpose motivate you to acheive the goals, you planned. That is why you need to understand for what you want to organize your time.

2) It is important to set not only short-term goals, for example for a day or a week, but also set long-term goals - for several months or for a year. This helps you to undestand which direction you are moving in.

3)You need to pay attention to the planning your personal time, when you can relax, meet your friends and relatives. If you have a rest, it will help you to work better.

So, I hope these tips will help you as they helped me.","expert_annotation":{"annotator_id":5,"errors":[{"text":"property","labels":["Lexical choice"],"start":96,"end":104},{"text":"rests.","labels":["Lexical choice"],"start":173,"end":179},{"text":"procces","labels":["Grammar"],"start":247,"end":254},{"text":"procces","labels":["Spelling"],"start":247,"end":254},{"text":" itself, but the purpose","labels":["Punctuation"],"start":266,"end":290},{"text":"motivate","labels":["Grammar"],"start":325,"end":333},{"text":"the goals, you planned","labels":["Punctuation"],"start":349,"end":371},{"text":"for what you want to organize your time.","labels":["Word order"],"start":408,"end":448},{"text":"organize ","labels":["Spelling"],"start":429,"end":438},{"text":"undestand","labels":["Spelling"],"start":619,"end":628},{"text":"to the planning","labels":["Grammar"],"start":693,"end":708}],"strengths":[{"text":"procces","labels":["Spelling ✓","Text organization ✓"],"start":247,"end":254},{"text":"procces","labels":["Spelling ✓","Text organization ✓"],"start":247,"end":254},{"text":" itself, but the purpose","labels":["Spelling ✓","Text organization ✓"],"start":266,"end":290},{"text":"motivate","labels":["Spelling ✓","Text organization ✓"],"start":325,"end":333},{"text":"the goals, you planned","labels":["Spelling ✓","Text organization ✓"],"start":349,"end":371},{"text":"for what you want to organize your time.","labels":["Spelling ✓","Text organization ✓"],"start":408,"end":448},{"text":"organize ","labels":["Spelling ✓","Text organization ✓"],"start":429,"end":438},{"text":"undestand","labels":["Spelling ✓"],"start":619,"end":628},{"text":"So,","labels":["Discourse markers ✓"],"start":836,"end":839}],"comments":[]}},{"ref_id":"sample_07013","cefr_level":"A2","task":"You read this post in the Telegram chat of first year students: It is so difficult to study in university! Write an answer to this post (10 sentences) and give some tips on how to organise your days to have more free time.","student_text":"Hi, im styduing in the MIPT - in one of the hardest technical universitiyes in Russia, and i want to give you some advices.

My first advidce - it to sleep from 7 to 9 hours (how much exactly depends from you) in day, and you should stand from up and go to bed in one time in day. With sleeping regulary in one time you will feel better and maybe you will feel better.

Next advice - it to make calendar with notions, or something like this, it helps to plan day wery succesfull. Sometimes procrastination wins you, but with notions it weel be easier to make hometasks in time.

The third advice - food delivery, if you have not enought time to cook something, or to go somewhere in big sity you can easily get food from delivery services. But sometimes people eats only food from delivery, and even don't go out from their rooms, of course it isn't good.

And the last what i want to say - probably your biggest troubles with time it that you have not hard character. Without this super ability it hard to self-controle, may be you should to ask your friends wathching what are you doing in free time, they can anounce if you spend time on games or other time-killers.

I believe that my advices can help to had more free time, good bye.","expert_annotation":{"annotator_id":5,"errors":[{"text":"im","labels":["Punctuation"],"start":4,"end":6},{"text":"styduing","labels":["Spelling"],"start":7,"end":15},{"text":"in the MIPT","labels":["Grammar"],"start":16,"end":27},{"text":" in","labels":["Grammar"],"start":29,"end":32},{"text":"i","labels":["Punctuation"],"start":91,"end":92},{"text":"some advices","labels":["Grammar"],"start":110,"end":122},{"text":"advidce","labels":["Grammar"],"start":134,"end":141},{"text":"advidce","labels":["Spelling"],"start":134,"end":141},{"text":"it to sleep","labels":["Grammar","Spelling"],"start":144,"end":155},{"text":"depends from","labels":["Grammar","Spelling"],"start":192,"end":204},{"text":"in day","labels":["Grammar"],"start":210,"end":216},{"text":"stand from up","labels":["Grammar"],"start":233,"end":246},{"text":"","labels":["Grammar","Lexical choice"],"start":261,"end":261},{"text":" in day.
